draft.introduction: 2 Timothy 2:1–13 exhorts Timothy to endure hardship and includes an early Christian hymn: “If we have died with him, we will also live with him.” The passage frames the Christian life as co-participation in Christ’s death and reign. From Codex Sinaiticus.

translation.text: You then, my child, be strengthened by the grace that is in Christ Jesus, and what you have heard from me in the presence of many witnesses entrust to faithful men, who will be able to teach others also. Share in suffering as a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
translation.original_text: συ ουν τεκνον μου ενδυναμου εν τη χαριτι τη εν χριστω ιησου

translation.text: The saying is trustworthy, for: If we have died with him, we will also live with him; if we endure, we will also reign with him; if we deny him, he also will deny us; if we are faithless, he remains faithful—for he cannot deny himself.
translation.original_text: πιστος ο λογος ει γαρ συναπεθανομεν και συζησομεν

translator_notes.note: The hymnic fragment uses four syn- (with) compounds: co-died, co-live, co-endure, co-reign. This “with Christ” language (συν-χριστος) is distinctly Pauline and expresses participation rather than mere imitation.
